title: Venture Portfolio iQube (v1.0)
description: PREMIUM, CUMULATIVE upload schema for Venture Lab Pro/Elite. It is a strict SUPERSET of the Venture Pro schema (v1.0): everything Pro has (operator, strategy, experienceModel, experienceGuide, standing, plan, and full 13-layer ventures) PLUS multiple ventures and a portfolio block (thesis, prioritisation, cross-venture intelligence). A Pro/Elite operator downloads and maintains ONLY this one file.

_agent_briefing.audience: An off-platform AI assistant helping a Pro/Elite operator prepare a Venture Portfolio iQube.
_agent_briefing.cumulative_design: Schemas are cumulative supersets: Venture Light (v0.5) ⊂ Venture Pro (v1.0) ⊂ Venture Portfolio (this file). A portfolio operator uploads this single file — it populates ExperienceModel, ExperienceGuide, Standing, every venture's full 13-layer VentureQube, AND the portfolio-level thesis + priority order. There is no need to also upload the Light or Pro files.
_agent_briefing.ventures: Each entry in `ventures[]` uses the SAME full 13-layer VentureQube shape defined in the Venture Pro schema (ventureQube-pro-schema.json → definitions.ventureProV1): identity, thesis, intent, signalEvidence, archetypes, revenueArchitecture, commercialModel, capability, resource, execution, delegation, outcome, governance, institutional. Pro/Elite may include multiple ventures (Pro up to 3, Elite unlimited — enforced server-side).
_agent_briefing.portfolio_block: The `portfolio` block is the portfolio-only KNOWLEDGE layer: a `thesis` that spans all ventures, an explicit `priorities` order (by venture name or slug), and free-form `notes`. Cross-venture intelligence (shared capabilities, stage spread) is DERIVED server-side from the ventures — you don't provide it.
_agent_briefing.operating_model_block: The OPTIONAL `operatingModel` block is the portfolio-only OPERATING layer — distinct from `portfolio`, which is knowledge. Where `portfolio.thesis` says WHY this portfolio exists, `operatingModel` says HOW it is run day to day (mission, successMetrics, activeObjectives, priorityPartners, priorityActions, reviewCadence, primaryMetric). This is what turns the portfolio into a living brief aigentMe executes against as Chief of Staff. Omit it unless the operator wants daily operating execution. Do NOT hardwire these operating fields into the core schema or into a venture — they belong only here.
_agent_briefing.what_to_produce: A single JSON file: the Pro base + the ventures array (full 13-layer each) + the portfolio block (+ optionally the operatingModel block).

experienceGuide.description: Same as Venture Light/Pro (sphere lattice + focusIntent).

operatingModel.description: REQUIRED operating-artifact layer at Portfolio level. A portfolio without an operating model is just a collection of ventures; with one it is an operating system aigentMe can execute daily as Chief of Staff. The core schema stays a general-purpose knowledge artifact; operations live here.

mission.description: The OPERATIONAL expression of portfolio.thesis — what we are doing right now in service of that Why (e.g. "Operation Leap"). There is no separate operating thesis; portfolio.thesis is the single Why, mission is the What-right-now.

successMetrics.description: Concrete portfolio success metrics — e.g. "4,000 Passport holders", "$100K MRR", "100 Founder Operators identified", "25 Founder Office conversions". These are outcomes.

activeObjectives.description: Cross-venture objectives currently in flight, each with a lifecycle status so aigentMe can distinguish active / completed / blocked / deferred.

priorityPartners.description: Strategic partners across the portfolio (e.g. Project Liberty, Horizon, Lamina1).

priorityActions.description: Next concrete actions across the portfolio, highest-priority first.

nextReviewDate.description: When the next review is due (ISO date). Cadence is how often; this is when — lets aigentMe auto-generate the next review brief.

primaryMetric.description: The portfolio north-star KPI. Layered framing: public mental model = Time-to-Value ("have our actions collapsed time to value?"); internal metric = Proof of Time Saved (PoTS); constitutional principle = Net Value Acceleration (Time-to-Value minus Risk Repair Burden). Aligns every venture: Passport → time to citizenship, aigentMe → time to agency, Standing → time to trust, Founder Office → time to venture readiness.

ventures.description: Each venture uses the FULL 13-layer VentureQube shape from the Venture Pro schema (definitions.ventureProV1). Multiple ventures allowed (Pro ≤3, Elite unlimited; enforced server-side).

portfolio.description: Portfolio-only layer. Cross-venture intelligence (sharedCapabilities, stageSpread) is derived server-side and is NOT provided here.

thesis.description: The throughline that ties all ventures together — why this portfolio, what it compounds toward.

priorities.description: Explicit prioritisation order, listing venture names or slugs from `ventures[]` highest-priority first.

notes.description: Prioritisation rationale, shared resources, sequencing across ventures.
